skill.name: note-summarizer
skill.description: Organize study notes into a structured knowledge-point outline and export to a .docx summary when you need a shareable, hierarchical document from Word/PPT/Text/Markdown inputs.
skill.summary: Convert study notes from multiple formats into a single structured .docx outline with key-point summaries per section.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 58 static analysis findings are false positives. The scanner pattern-matched markdown code blocks as shell execution, local dict .get() calls as environment access, source reference URLs as network calls, and image library constants as cryptographic algorithms. The actual code is a legitimate document processing script that reads local files and exports a .docx with no network access, no external command execution, and no credential handling.

low_findings.title: Markdown code blocks flagged as shell backtick execution
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: 37 static findings flagged backtick characters in SKILL.md as Ruby/shell execution. All instances are markdown fenced code blocks (```bash) showing example commands for documentation. No actual shell execution occurs.

low_findings.title: Local dict access flagged as environment variable access
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: 7 static findings flagged config.get() calls as 'Configuration library' env_access. These are accesses to a local Python dict object containing user-provided parameters (input paths, settings), not os.environ or system configuration.

low_findings.title: Source reference URL flagged as network call
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: 2 static findings flagged a hardcoded URL in SKILL.md as a network indicator. The URL is a source reference link to the skill's GitHub repository (line 7), not a network request executed at runtime.

low_findings.title: Image processing library calls flagged as weak cryptographic algorithm
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Scanner flagged Image.LANCZOS (resampling filter), Presentation() (PPTX loader), and ArgumentParser() as 'Weak cryptographic algorithm'. These are standard library calls for image processing and argument parsing, not cryptographic operations.

low_findings.title: JSON data in audit report flagged as system reconnaissance
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Scanner flagged test result data in note-summarizer_audit_result_v1.json as 'System reconnaissance'. The JSON file contains evaluation metadata and test results from a prior audit, not active reconnaissance commands.

low_findings.title: Output discipline documentation flagged as system reconnaissance
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: SKILL.md line 50 ('keep results reproducible, identify assumptions explicitly') was flagged as reconnaissance. This is a best-practices documentation line about output discipline, not a system information gathering instruction.

content.user_title: Organize Study Notes into Structured .docx Outlines
content.value_statement: Study notes from different formats (Word, PowerPoint, text, markdown) are hard to review in a unified way. This skill extracts key points from each source, preserves heading structure, and exports a single .docx outline with bullet-point summaries per section.
actual_capabilities: Read and extract text content from .docx, .pptx, .txt, and .md input files
Generate bullet-point key summaries per section with configurable maximum limit
Preserve heading hierarchy (Heading 1, 2, 3) from source documents in the output outline
Copy images from Word and PowerPoint documents into an appendix section of the output
Export a formatted .docx document with structured outline and section-based summaries
Run with a JSON configuration file for reproducible batch processing
limitations: Does not connect to external AI services or APIs for intelligent summarization
Does not support PDF files as input sources
Does not extract images from plain text or markdown files
Image size is configurable but large or numerous images may increase output file size

use_cases.title: Consolidate lecture notes from multiple formats
use_cases.description: A student has class notes in Word documents, PowerPoint slides, and text files. This skill reads all sources, extracts headings and content, and produces one organized .docx outline with summaries per topic.

use_cases.title: Create a shareable research paper outline
use_cases.description: A researcher has notes from papers in markdown and text format. This skill compiles them into a structured .docx document with clear sections and bullet-point takeaways for sharing with collaborators.

use_cases.title: Organize meeting notes into a summary document
use_cases.description: A professional has meeting notes in various formats (PPT presentations, text notes, Word documents). This skill merges them into one hierarchical .docx with key points per agenda item.

prompt_templates.title: Basic single-file note summarization
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the note-summarizer skill to process my study notes file at [path/to/notes.docx] and create a summary outline as [output/summary.docx]. Keep the heading structure and limit key points to 5 per section.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has one document they need summarized

prompt_templates.title: Multi-source note consolidation
prompt_templates.prompt: I have study materials in multiple files: [path/to/lecture.docx], [path/to/slides.pptx], and [path/to/notes.md]. Use the note-summarizer skill to combine them into one outline at [output/study-guide.docx]. Preserve headings and extract images from the Word and PowerPoint files.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to merge notes from different formats

prompt_templates.title: Custom configuration with JSON input
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a JSON config file for the note-summarizer skill with inputs [notes.docx, lecture.pptx], output path [output/summary.docx], keep_headings enabled, max_bullets_per_section set to 8, and image_mode set to copy. Then run the script with this config.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants reproducible batch processing with defined settings

prompt_templates.title: Quick text-only outline without images
prompt_templates.prompt: Use note-summarizer to process [path/to/notes.md] and generate a text-only outline at [output/outline.docx]. Skip images, keep headings, and show up to 3 key points per section. Process the file locally without any network calls.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs a lightweight, fast outline from plain text

output_examples.input: A Word document with three sections: Introduction (H1), Methods (H1), Results (H1), each containing multiple paragraphs of study notes.
output: - Overview of key theories covered in lecture
- Tools and instruments used in the study
- Notable patterns observed in the data

output_examples.input: A mix of a Word document (lecture_week1.docx) and a PowerPoint presentation (slides_week1.pptx) covering the same topic.
output: - Combined outline with headings from both sources merged in order
- Bullet-point summaries (max 6 per section) extracted from both files
- An appendix section with images copied from the PowerPoint slides

best_practices: Organize source documents with clear heading hierarchies (Heading 1, 2, 3) before processing to get well-structured output outlines.
Use the JSON configuration mode for repeatable workflows: create an input.json file with paths and settings, then run the same config on updated source files.
Set max_bullets_per_section to a reasonable limit (3-6) based on content density to avoid overly long or too sparse summaries.
anti_patterns: Do not expect AI-powered semantic understanding or abstractive summarization. This skill extracts and organizes existing content into an outline format without rewriting or interpreting meaning.
Do not pass PDF files as input. The skill only supports .docx, .pptx, .txt, and .md formats. Use a separate conversion tool first if your source is in PDF format.
Do not rely on this skill for confidential data redaction or sensitive content filtering. All input text passes through to the output; no content analysis or screening is performed.

faq.question: What input file formats does the note-summarizer support?
faq.answer: The skill supports .docx (Word), .pptx (PowerPoint), .txt (plain text), and .md (markdown) files. PDF files are not supported.

faq.question: Does this skill use AI to summarize the content?
faq.answer: No. The skill extracts and organizes existing text into a structured outline format. It does not rewrite, interpret, or generate new content using AI models.

faq.question: Can I include images from my source documents in the output?
faq.answer: Yes. Set image_mode to 'copy' in the configuration. Images from Word and PowerPoint documents are placed in an appendix section. Images from text and markdown files are not supported.

faq.question: Is the output .docx file editable after generation?
faq.answer: Yes. The output is a standard Word .docx file that can be opened and edited in Microsoft Word, Google Docs, LibreOffice, or any compatible word processor.

faq.question: Can I process files located in different folders?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide absolute or relative file paths in the inputs array. The skill resolves paths from the current working directory where the script is executed.

faq.question: Does this skill make any network requests?
faq.answer: No. All processing happens locally on your machine. The skill reads files from your local filesystem and writes the output to a local path. No data is sent over the network.
